     8	#ifndef BE_OBJECT_H
     9	#define BE_OBJECT_H
    10	
    11	#include "berry.h"
    12	
    13	/* basic types, do not change value */
    14	#define BE_NONE         (-1)    /* unknown type */
    15	#define BE_COMPTR       (-2)    /* common pointer */
    16	#define BE_INDEX        (-3)    /* index for instance variable, previously BE_INT */
    17	#define BE_NIL          0
    18	#define BE_INT          1
    19	#define BE_REAL         2
    20	#define BE_BOOL         3
    21	#define BE_FUNCTION     4
    22	#define BE_STRING       5       /* from this type can be gced, see BE_GCOBJECT */
    23	#define BE_CLASS        6
    24	#define BE_INSTANCE     7
    25	#define BE_PROTO        8
    26	#define BE_LIST         9
    27	#define BE_MAP          10
    28	#define BE_MODULE       11
    29	#define BE_COMOBJ       12      /* common object */
    30	#define BE_NTVFUNC      ((0 << 5) | BE_FUNCTION)
    31	#define BE_CLOSURE      ((1 << 5) | BE_FUNCTION)
    32	#define BE_NTVCLOS      ((2 << 5) | BE_FUNCTION)
    33	
    34	#define array_count(a)   (sizeof(a) / sizeof((a)[0]))
    35	
    36	#define bcommon_header          \
    37	    struct bgcobject *next;     \
    38	    bbyte type;                 \
    39	    bbyte marked
    40	
    41	#define bstring_header          \
    42	    bcommon_header;             \
    43	    bbyte extra;                \
    44	    bbyte slen
    45	
    46	typedef struct bgcobject {
    47	    bcommon_header;
    48	} bgcobject;
    49	
    50	typedef struct bclosure bclosure;
    51	typedef struct bntvclos bntvclos;
    52	typedef struct bclass bclass;
    53	typedef struct binstance binstance;
    54	typedef struct blist blist;
    55	typedef struct bmap bmap;
    56	typedef struct bupval bupval;
    57	
    58	typedef uint32_t binstruction;
    59	
    60	typedef struct bstring {
    61	    bstring_header;
    62	} bstring;
    63	
    64	/* the definition of the vector and stack data structures.
    65	 * in fact, the stack is implemented by vector. */
    66	typedef struct bvector {
    67	    int capacity;   /* the number of elements that the vector can store */
    68	    int size;       /* the size of each element (bytes) */
    69	    int count;      /* number of elements of the vector */
    70	    void *data;     /* the data block pointer, if vector is empty,
    71	                       it will point to the first element */
    72	    void *end;      /* pointer to the last element, if the vector is empty,
    73	                       the end pointer will be smaller than the data pointer */
    74	} bvector, bstack;
    75	
    76	/* berry value data union, a berry value is always described
    77	 * by the data structure contained in the bvaldata union. */
    78	union bvaldata {
    79	    bbool b;        /* boolean */
    80	    breal r;        /* real number */
    81	    bint i;         /* integer number */
    82	    void *p;        /* object pointer */
    83	    const void *c;  /* const object pointer */
    84	    bstring *s;     /* string pointer */
    85	    bgcobject *gc;  /* GC object */
    86	    bntvfunc nf;    /* native C function */
    87	#ifdef __cplusplus
    88	    BE_CONSTEXPR bvaldata(bbool v) : b(v) {}
    89	    BE_CONSTEXPR bvaldata(breal v) : r(v) {}
    90	    BE_CONSTEXPR bvaldata(bint v) : i(v) {}
    91	    BE_CONSTEXPR bvaldata(void *v) : p(v) {}
    92	    BE_CONSTEXPR bvaldata(const void *v) : c(v) {}
    93	    BE_CONSTEXPR bvaldata(bntvfunc v) : nf(v) {}
    94	#endif
    95	};
    96	
    97	/* berry value. for simple types, the value of the data is stored,
    98	 * while the complex type stores a reference to the data. */
    99	typedef struct bvalue {
   100	    union bvaldata v; /* the value data */
   101	    int type;         /* the value type */
   102	} bvalue;
   103	
   104	typedef struct {
   105	#if BE_DEBUG_VAR_INFO
   106	    bstring *name;
   107	#endif
   108	    bbyte instack;
   109	    bbyte idx;
   110	} bupvaldesc;
   111	
   112	typedef struct {
   113	#if BE_DEBUG_RUNTIME_INFO > 1
   114	    uint16_t linenumber;
   115	    uint16_t endpc;
   116	#else
   117	    int linenumber;
   118	    int endpc;
   119	#endif
   120	} blineinfo;
   121	
   122	typedef struct {
   123	    bstring *name;
   124	#if BE_DEBUG_RUNTIME_INFO > 1
   125	    uint16_t beginpc;
   126	    uint16_t endpc;
   127	#else
   128	    int beginpc;
   129	    int endpc;
   130	#endif
   131	} bvarinfo;
   132	
   133	typedef struct bproto {
   134	    bcommon_header;
   135	    bbyte nstack; /* number of stack size by this function */
   136	    bbyte nupvals; /* upvalue count */
   137	    bbyte argc; /* argument count */
   138	    bbyte varg; /* variable argument position + 1 */
   139	    bgcobject *gray; /* for gc gray list */
   140	    bupvaldesc *upvals;
   141	    bvalue *ktab; /* constants table */
   142	    struct bproto **ptab; /* proto table */
   143	    binstruction *code; /* instructions sequence */
   144	    bstring *name; /* function name */
   145	    int codesize; /* code size */
   146	    int nconst; /* constants count */
   147	    int nproto; /* proto count */
   148	    bstring *source; /* source file name */
   149	#if BE_DEBUG_RUNTIME_INFO /* debug information */
   150	    blineinfo *lineinfo;
   151	    int nlineinfo;
   152	#endif
   153	#if BE_DEBUG_VAR_INFO
   154	    bvarinfo *varinfo;
   155	    int nvarinfo;
   156	#endif
   157	} bproto;
   158	
   159	/* berry closure */
   160	struct bclosure {
   161	    bcommon_header;
   162	    bbyte nupvals;
   163	    bgcobject *gray; /* for gc gray list */
   164	    bproto *proto;
   165	    bupval *upvals[1];
   166	};
   167	
   168	/* C native function or closure */
   169	struct bntvclos {
   170	    bcommon_header;
   171	    bbyte nupvals;
   172	    bgcobject *gray; /* for gc gray list */
   173	    bntvfunc f;
   174	};
   175	
   176	/* common object */
   177	typedef struct {
   178	    bcommon_header;
   179	    void *data;
   180	    bntvfunc destory;
   181	} bcommomobj;
   182	
   183	typedef const char* (*breader)(void*, size_t*);
   184	
   185	#define cast(_T, _v)            ((_T)(_v))
   186	#define cast_int(_v)            cast(int, _v)
   187	#define cast_bool(_v)           cast(bbool, _v)
   188	#define basetype(_t)            ((_t) & 0x1F)
   189	
   190	#define var_type(_v)            ((_v)->type)
   191	#define var_basetype(_v)        basetype((_v)->type)
   192	#define var_istype(_v, _t)      (var_type(_v) == _t)
   193	#define var_settype(_v, _t)     ((_v)->type = _t)
   194	#define var_setobj(_v, _t, _o)  { (_v)->v.p = _o; var_settype(_v, _t); }
   195	
   196	#define var_isnil(_v)           var_istype(_v, BE_NIL)
   197	#define var_isbool(_v)          var_istype(_v, BE_BOOL)
   198	#define var_isint(_v)           var_istype(_v, BE_INT)
   199	#define var_isreal(_v)          var_istype(_v, BE_REAL)
   200	#define var_isstr(_v)           var_istype(_v, BE_STRING)
   201	#define var_isclosure(_v)       var_istype(_v, BE_CLOSURE)
   202	#define var_isntvclos(_v)       var_istype(_v, BE_NTVCLOS)
   203	#define var_isntvfunc(_v)       var_istype(_v, BE_NTVFUNC)
   204	#define var_isfunction(_v)      (var_basetype(_v) == BE_FUNCTION)
   205	#define var_isproto(_v)         var_istype(_v, BE_PROTO)
   206	#define var_isclass(_v)         var_istype(_v, BE_CLASS)
   207	#define var_isinstance(_v)      var_istype(_v, BE_INSTANCE)
   208	#define var_islist(_v)          var_istype(_v, BE_LIST)
   209	#define var_ismap(_v)           var_istype(_v, BE_MAP)
   210	#define var_ismodule(_v)        var_istype(_v, BE_MODULE)
   211	#define var_isindex(_v)         var_istype(_v, BE_INDEX)
   212	#define var_iscomptr(_v)        var_istype(_v, BE_COMPTR)
   213	#define var_isnumber(_v)        (var_isint(_v) || var_isreal(_v))
   214	
   215	#define var_setnil(_v)          var_settype(_v, BE_NIL)
   216	#define var_setval(_v, _s)      (*(_v) = *(_s))
   217	#define var_setbool(_v, _b)     { var_settype(_v, BE_BOOL); (_v)->v.b = (bbool)(_b); }
   218	#define var_setint(_v, _i)      { var_settype(_v, BE_INT); (_v)->v.i = (_i); }
   219	#define var_setreal(_v, _r)     { var_settype(_v, BE_REAL); (_v)->v.r = (_r); }
   220	#define var_setstr(_v, _s)      var_setobj(_v, BE_STRING, _s)
   221	#define var_setinstance(_v, _o) var_setobj(_v, BE_INSTANCE, _o)
   222	#define var_setclass(_v, _o)    var_setobj(_v, BE_CLASS, _o)
   223	#define var_setclosure(_v, _o)  var_setobj(_v, BE_CLOSURE, _o)
   224	#define var_setntvclos(_v, _o)  var_setobj(_v, BE_NTVCLOS, _o)
   225	#define var_setntvfunc(_v, _o)  { (_v)->v.nf = (_o); var_settype(_v, BE_NTVFUNC); }
   226	#define var_setlist(_v, _o)     var_setobj(_v, BE_LIST, _o)
   227	#define var_setmap(_v, _o)      var_setobj(_v, BE_MAP, _o)
   228	#define var_setmodule(_v, _o)   var_setobj(_v, BE_MODULE, _o)
   229	#define var_setindex(_v, _i)      { var_settype(_v, BE_INDEX); (_v)->v.i = (_i); }
   230	#define var_setproto(_v, _o)    var_setobj(_v, BE_PROTO, _o)
   231	
   232	#define var_tobool(_v)          ((_v)->v.b)
   233	#define var_toint(_v)           ((_v)->v.i)
   234	#define var_toreal(_v)          ((_v)->v.r)
   235	#define var_tostr(_v)           ((_v)->v.s)
   236	#define var_togc(_v)            ((_v)->v.gc)
   237	#define var_toobj(_v)           ((_v)->v.p)
   238	#define var_tontvfunc(_v)       ((_v)->v.nf)
   239	#define var_toidx(_v)           cast_int(var_toint(_v))
   240	
   241	const char* be_vtype2str(bvalue *v);
   242	bvalue* be_indexof(bvm *vm, int idx);
   243	void be_commonobj_delete(bvm *vm, bgcobject *obj);
   244	
   245	#endif
</output>

    14	/* protected-call function */

    23	
    24	#define be_stackpop(vm, n)      ((vm)->top -= (n))
    25	
    26	/* in MinGW-w64, setjmp / longjmp may be broken,
    27	 * so here is replaced by __builtin version. */
    28	#if defined(__GNUC__) && defined(__MINGW32__)
    29	  #define be_setjmp(env)        __builtin_setjmp(env)

     8	#ifndef BE_PARSER_H
     9	#define BE_PARSER_H
    10	
    11	#include "be_object.h"
    12	#include "be_string.h"
    13	
    14	typedef enum {
    15	    ETVOID,    /* unknown (new variable or error) */
    16	    ETNIL,
    17	    ETBOOL,
    18	    ETREAL,
    19	    ETINT,
    20	    ETSTRING,
    21	    ETPROTO,
    22	    ETCONST,
    23	    ETLOCAL,    /* local variable, allocated until end of scope */
    24	    ETGLOBAL,   /* global by index number */
    25	    ETUPVAL,
    26	    ETMEMBER,   /* member accessor (by name) */
    27	    ETINDEX,    /* index accessor (ex array index) */
    28	    ETREG,      /* temporary register, can be freed if top of stack */
    29	    ETNGLOBAL   /* named global */
    30	} exptype_t;
    31	
    32	typedef struct {
    33	    union {
    34	        struct { /* for suffix */
    35	            unsigned int idx:9; /* suffix RK index */
    36	            unsigned int obj:9; /* object RK index */
    37	            unsigned int tt:5; /* object type */
    38	        } ss;
    39	        breal r;     /* for ETREAL */
    40	        bint i;      /* for ETINT */
    41	        bstring *s;  /* for ETSTRING */
    42	        bproto *p;  /* for ETPROTO */
    43	        int idx;     /* variable index */
    44	    } v;
    45	    int t; /* patch list of 'exit when true' */
    46	    int f; /* patch list of 'exit when false' */
    47	    bbyte not; /* not mark */
    48	    bbyte type;
    49	} bexpdesc;
    50	
    51	typedef struct bblockinfo {
    52	    struct bblockinfo *prev;
    53	    bbyte nactlocals; /* number of active local variables */
    54	    bbyte type;       /* block type mask */
    55	    bbyte hasupval;   /* has upvalue mark */
    56	    int breaklist;    /* break list */
    57	    int beginpc;      /* begin pc */
    58	    int continuelist; /* continue list */
    59	} bblockinfo;
    60	
    61	typedef struct bfuncinfo {
    62	    struct bfuncinfo *prev; /* outer function */
    63	    bproto *proto; /* the function prototype */
    64	    bblockinfo *binfo; /* block information */
    65	    struct blexer *lexer; /* the lexer pointer */
    66	    blist *local; /* local variable */
    67	    bmap *upval; /* upvalue variable */
    68	    bvector code; /* code vector */
    69	    bvector kvec; /* constants table */
    70	    bvector pvec; /* proto table */
    71	#if BE_DEBUG_RUNTIME_INFO /* debug information */
    72	    bvector linevec;
    73	#endif
    74	#if BE_DEBUG_VAR_INFO
    75	    bvector varvec;
    76	#endif
    77	    int pc; /* program count */
    78	    bbyte freereg; /* first free register */
    79	    bbyte flags; /* some flages */
    80	} bfuncinfo;
    81	
    82	/* code block type definitions */
    83	#define BLOCK_LOOP      1
    84	#define BLOCK_EXCEPT    2
    85	
    86	bclosure *be_parser_source(bvm *vm,
    87	    const char *fname, breader reader, void *data, bbool islocal);
    88	
    89	#endif
</output>

     8	#ifndef BE_VM_H
     9	#define BE_VM_H
    10	
    11	#include "be_object.h"
    12	
    13	#define comp_is_named_gbl(vm)       ((vm)->compopt & (1<<COMP_NAMED_GBL))
    14	#define comp_set_named_gbl(vm)      ((vm)->compopt |= (1<<COMP_NAMED_GBL))
    15	#define comp_clear_named_gbl(vm)    ((vm)->compopt &= ~(1<<COMP_NAMED_GBL))
    16	
    17	#define comp_is_strict(vm)       ((vm)->compopt & (1<<COMP_STRICT))
    18	#define comp_set_strict(vm)      ((vm)->compopt |= (1<<COMP_STRICT))
    19	#define comp_clear_strict(vm)    ((vm)->compopt &= ~(1<<COMP_STRICT))
    20	
    21	/* Compilation options */
    22	typedef enum {
    23	    COMP_NAMED_GBL = 0x00, /* compile with named globals */
    24	    COMP_STRICT = 0x01, /* compile with named globals */
    25	} compoptmask;
    26	
    27	typedef struct {
    28	    struct {
    29	        bmap *vtab; /* global variable index table */
    30	        bvector vlist; /* global variable list */
    31	    } global;
    32	    struct {
    33	        bmap *vtab; /* built-in variable index table */
    34	        bvector vlist; /* built-in variable list */
    35	    } builtin;
    36	} bglobaldesc;
    37	
    38	typedef struct {
    39	    bvalue *func; /* function register pointer */
    40	    bvalue *top; /* top register pointer */
    41	    bvalue *reg; /* base register pointer */
    42	    binstruction *ip; /* instruction pointer (only berry-function) */
    43	#if BE_USE_DEBUG_HOOK
    44	    blineinfo *lineinfo;
    45	#endif
    46	    int status;
    47	} bcallframe;
    48	
    49	struct bgc {
    50	    bgcobject *list; /* the GC-object list */
    51	    bgcobject *gray; /* the gray object list */
    52	    bgcobject *fixed; /* the fixed objecct list  */
    53	    size_t usage; /* the count of bytes currently allocated */
    54	    size_t threshold; /* he threshold of allocation for the next GC */
    55	    bbyte steprate; /* the rate of increase in the distribution between two GCs (percentage) */
    56	    bbyte status;
    57	};
    58	
    59	struct bstringtable {
    60	    bstring **table;
    61	    int count; /* string count */
    62	    int size;
    63	};
    64	
    65	struct bmoduledesc {
    66	    bmap *loaded; /* loaded module map */
    67	    blist *path; /* module load path list */
    68	};
    69	
    70	typedef struct {
    71	    bvalue func;
    72	    binstruction *ip;
    73	} bcallsnapshot;
    74	
    75	struct bupval {
    76	    bvalue* value;
    77	    union {
    78	        bvalue value;
    79	        struct bupval* next;
    80	    } u;
    81	    int refcnt;
    82	};
    83	
    84	struct bvm {
    85	    bglobaldesc gbldesc; /* global description */
    86	    bvalue *stack; /* stack space */
    87	    bvalue *stacktop; /* stack top register */
    88	    bupval *upvalist; /* open upvalue list */
    89	    bstack callstack; /* function call stack */
    90	    bstack exceptstack; /* exception stack */
    91	    bcallframe *cf; /* function call frame */
    92	    bvalue *reg; /* function base register */
    93	    bvalue *top; /* function top register */
    94	    binstruction *ip; /* function instruction pointer */
    95	    struct blongjmp *errjmp; /* error jump point */
    96	    bstack refstack; /* object reference stack */
    97	    struct bmoduledesc module; /* module description */
    98	    struct bstringtable strtab; /* short string table */
    99	    bstack tracestack; /* call state trace-stack */
   100	    bmap *ntvclass; /* native class table */
   101	    blist *registry; /* registry list */
   102	    struct bgc gc;
   103	    bbyte compopt; /* compilation options */
   104	#if BE_USE_OBSERVABILITY_HOOK
   105	    bobshook obshook;
   106	#endif
   107	#if BE_USE_DEBUG_HOOK
   108	    bvalue hook;
   109	    bbyte hookmask;
   110	#endif
   111	};
   112	
   113	#define NONE_FLAG           0
   114	#define BASE_FRAME          (1 << 0)
   115	#define PRIM_FUNC           (1 << 1)
   116	
   117	void be_dofunc(bvm *vm, bvalue *v, int argc);
   118	bbool be_value2bool(bvm *vm, bvalue *v);

     1	/********************************************************************
     2	** Copyright (c) 2018-2020 Guan Wenliang

     4	** skiars@qq.com, https://github.com/Skiars/berry

     7	********************************************************************/
     8	#ifndef BE_OBJECT_H
     9	#define BE_OBJECT_H
    10	
    11	#include "berry.h"
    12	
    13	/* basic types, do not change value */
    14	#define BE_NONE         (-1)    /* unknown type */
    15	#define BE_COMPTR       (-2)    /* common pointer */
    16	#define BE_INDEX        (-3)    /* index for instance variable, previously BE_INT */
    17	#define BE_NIL          0
    18	#define BE_INT          1
    19	#define BE_REAL         2
    20	#define BE_BOOL         3
    21	#define BE_FUNCTION     4
    22	#define BE_STRING       5       /* from this type can be gced, see BE_GCOBJECT */
    23	#define BE_CLASS        6
    24	#define BE_INSTANCE     7
    25	#define BE_PROTO        8
    26	#define BE_LIST         9
    27	#define BE_MAP          10
    28	#define BE_MODULE       11
    29	#define BE_COMOBJ       12      /* common object */
    30	#define BE_NTVFUNC      ((0 << 5) | BE_FUNCTION)
    31	#define BE_CLOSURE      ((1 << 5) | BE_FUNCTION)
    32	#define BE_NTVCLOS      ((2 << 5) | BE_FUNCTION)
    33	
    34	#define array_count(a)   (sizeof(a) / sizeof((a)[0]))
    35	
    36	#define bcommon_header          \
    37	    struct bgcobject *next;     \
    38	    bbyte type;                 \
    39	    bbyte marked
    40	
    41	#define bstring_header          \
    42	    bcommon_header;             \
    43	    bbyte extra;                \
    44	    bbyte slen
    45	
    46	typedef struct bgcobject {
    47	    bcommon_header;
    48	} bgcobject;
    49	
    50	typedef struct bclosure bclosure;
    51	typedef struct bntvclos bntvclos;
    52	typedef struct bclass bclass;
    53	typedef struct binstance binstance;
    54	typedef struct blist blist;
    55	typedef struct bmap bmap;
    56	typedef struct bupval bupval;
    57	
    58	typedef uint32_t binstruction;
    59	
    60	typedef struct bstring {
    61	    bstring_header;
    62	} bstring;
    63	
    64	/* the definition of the vector and stack data structures.
    65	 * in fact, the stack is implemented by vector. */
    66	typedef struct bvector {
    67	    int capacity;   /* the number of elements that the vector can store */
    68	    int size;       /* the size of each element (bytes) */
    69	    int count;      /* number of elements of the vector */
    70	    void *data;     /* the data block pointer, if vector is empty,
    71	                       it will point to the first element */
    72	    void *end;      /* pointer to the last element, if the vector is empty,
    73	                       the end pointer will be smaller than the data pointer */
    74	} bvector, bstack;
    75	
    76	/* berry value data union, a berry value is always described
    77	 * by the data structure contained in the bvaldata union. */
    78	union bvaldata {
    79	    bbool b;        /* boolean */
    80	    breal r;        /* real number */
    81	    bint i;         /* integer number */
    82	    void *p;        /* object pointer */
    83	    const void *c;  /* const object pointer */
    84	    bstring *s;     /* string pointer */
    85	    bgcobject *gc;  /* GC object */
    86	    bntvfunc nf;    /* native C function */
    87	#ifdef __cplusplus
    88	    BE_CONSTEXPR bvaldata(bbool v) : b(v) {}
    89	    BE_CONSTEXPR bvaldata(breal v) : r(v) {}
    90	    BE_CONSTEXPR bvaldata(bint v) : i(v) {}
    91	    BE_CONSTEXPR bvaldata(void *v) : p(v) {}
    92	    BE_CONSTEXPR bvaldata(const void *v) : c(v) {}
    93	    BE_CONSTEXPR bvaldata(bntvfunc v) : nf(v) {}
    94	#endif
    95	};
    96	
    97	/* berry value. for simple types, the value of the data is stored,
    98	 * while the complex type stores a reference to the data. */
    99	typedef struct bvalue {
   100	    union bvaldata v; /* the value data */
   101	    int type;         /* the value type */
   102	} bvalue;
   103	
   104	typedef struct {
   105	#if BE_DEBUG_VAR_INFO
   106	    bstring *name;
   107	#endif
   108	    bbyte instack;
   109	    bbyte idx;
   110	} bupvaldesc;
   111	
   112	typedef struct {
   113	#if BE_DEBUG_RUNTIME_INFO > 1
   114	    uint16_t linenumber;
   115	    uint16_t endpc;
   116	#else
   117	    int linenumber;
   118	    int endpc;
   119	#endif
   120	} blineinfo;
   121	
   122	typedef struct {
   123	    bstring *name;
   124	#if BE_DEBUG_RUNTIME_INFO > 1
   125	    uint16_t beginpc;
   126	    uint16_t endpc;
   127	#else
   128	    int beginpc;
   129	    int endpc;
   130	#endif
   131	} bvarinfo;
   132	
   133	typedef struct bproto {
   134	    bcommon_header;
   135	    bbyte nstack; /* number of stack size by this function */
   136	    bbyte nupvals; /* upvalue count */
   137	    bbyte argc; /* argument count */
   138	    bbyte varg; /* variable argument position + 1 */
   139	    bgcobject *gray; /* for gc gray list */
   140	    bupvaldesc *upvals;
   141	    bvalue *ktab; /* constants table */
   142	    struct bproto **ptab; /* proto table */
   143	    binstruction *code; /* instructions sequence */
   144	    bstring *name; /* function name */
   145	    int codesize; /* code size */
   146	    int nconst; /* constants count */
   147	    int nproto; /* proto count */
   148	    bstring *source; /* source file name */
   149	#if BE_DEBUG_RUNTIME_INFO /* debug information */
   150	    blineinfo *lineinfo;
   151	    int nlineinfo;
   152	#endif
   153	#if BE_DEBUG_VAR_INFO
   154	    bvarinfo *varinfo;
   155	    int nvarinfo;
   156	#endif
   157	} bproto;
   158	
   159	/* berry closure */
   160	struct bclosure {
   161	    bcommon_header;
   162	    bbyte nupvals;
   163	    bgcobject *gray; /* for gc gray list */
   164	    bproto *proto;
   165	    bupval *upvals[1];
   166	};
   167	
   168	/* C native function or closure */
   169	struct bntvclos {
   170	    bcommon_header;
   171	    bbyte nupvals;
   172	    bgcobject *gray; /* for gc gray list */
   173	    bntvfunc f;
   174	};
   175	
   176	/* common object */
   177	typedef struct {
   178	    bcommon_header;
   179	    void *data;
   180	    bntvfunc destory;
   181	} bcommomobj;
   182	
   183	typedef const char* (*breader)(void*, size_t*);
   184	
   185	#define cast(_T, _v)            ((_T)(_v))
   186	#define cast_int(_v)            cast(int, _v)
   187	#define cast_bool(_v)           cast(bbool, _v)
   188	#define basetype(_t)            ((_t) & 0x1F)
   189	
   190	#define var_type(_v)            ((_v)->type)
   191	#define var_basetype(_v)        basetype((_v)->type)
   192	#define var_istype(_v, _t)      (var_type(_v) == _t)
   193	#define var_settype(_v, _t)     ((_v)->type = _t)
   194	#define var_setobj(_v, _t, _o)  { (_v)->v.p = _o; var_settype(_v, _t); }
   195	
   196	#define var_isnil(_v)           var_istype(_v, BE_NIL)
   197	#define var_isbool(_v)          var_istype(_v, BE_BOOL)
   198	#define var_isint(_v)           var_istype(_v, BE_INT)
   199	#define var_isreal(_v)          var_istype(_v, BE_REAL)
   200	#define var_isstr(_v)           var_istype(_v, BE_STRING)
   201	#define var_isclosure(_v)       var_istype(_v, BE_CLOSURE)
   202	#define var_isntvclos(_v)       var_istype(_v, BE_NTVCLOS)
   203	#define var_isntvfunc(_v)       var_istype(_v, BE_NTVFUNC)
   204	#define var_isfunction(_v)      (var_basetype(_v) == BE_FUNCTION)
   205	#define var_isproto(_v)         var_istype(_v, BE_PROTO)
   206	#define var_isclass(_v)         var_istype(_v, BE_CLASS)
   207	#define var_isinstance(_v)      var_istype(_v, BE_INSTANCE)
   208	#define var_islist(_v)          var_istype(_v, BE_LIST)
   209	#define var_ismap(_v)           var_istype(_v, BE_MAP)
   210	#define var_ismodule(_v)        var_istype(_v, BE_MODULE)
   211	#define var_isindex(_v)         var_istype(_v, BE_INDEX)
   212	#define var_iscomptr(_v)        var_istype(_v, BE_COMPTR)
   213	#define var_isnumber(_v)        (var_isint(_v) || var_isreal(_v))
   214	
   215	#define var_setnil(_v)          var_settype(_v, BE_NIL)
   216	#define var_setval(_v, _s)      (*(_v) = *(_s))
   217	#define var_setbool(_v, _b)     { var_settype(_v, BE_BOOL); (_v)->v.b = (bbool)(_b); }
   218	#define var_setint(_v, _i)      { var_settype(_v, BE_INT); (_v)->v.i = (_i); }
   219	#define var_setreal(_v, _r)     { var_settype(_v, BE_REAL); (_v)->v.r = (_r); }
   220	#define var_setstr(_v, _s)      var_setobj(_v, BE_STRING, _s)
   221	#define var_setinstance(_v, _o) var_setobj(_v, BE_INSTANCE, _o)
   222	#define var_setclass(_v, _o)    var_setobj(_v, BE_CLASS, _o)
   223	#define var_setclosure(_v, _o)  var_setobj(_v, BE_CLOSURE, _o)
   224	#define var_setntvclos(_v, _o)  var_setobj(_v, BE_NTVCLOS, _o)
   225	#define var_setntvfunc(_v, _o)  { (_v)->v.nf = (_o); var_settype(_v, BE_NTVFUNC); }
   226	#define var_setlist(_v, _o)     var_setobj(_v, BE_LIST, _o)
   227	#define var_setmap(_v, _o)      var_setobj(_v, BE_MAP, _o)
   228	#define var_setmodule(_v, _o)   var_setobj(_v, BE_MODULE, _o)
   229	#define var_setindex(_v, _i)      { var_settype(_v, BE_INDEX); (_v)->v.i = (_i); }

     8	#ifndef BE_PARSER_H

    10	
    11	#include "be_object.h"
    12	#include "be_string.h"
    13	
    14	typedef enum {
    15	    ETVOID,    /* unknown (new variable or error) */
    16	    ETNIL,
    17	    ETBOOL,
    18	    ETREAL,
    19	    ETINT,
    20	    ETSTRING,
    21	    ETPROTO,
    22	    ETCONST,
    23	    ETLOCAL,    /* local variable, allocated until end of scope */
    24	    ETGLOBAL,   /* global by index number */
    25	    ETUPVAL,
    26	    ETMEMBER,   /* member accessor (by name) */
    27	    ETINDEX,    /* index accessor (ex array index) */
    28	    ETREG,      /* temporary register, can be freed if top of stack */
    29	    ETNGLOBAL   /* named global */
    30	} exptype_t;
    31	
    32	typedef struct {
    33	    union {
    34	        struct { /* for suffix */
    35	            unsigned int idx:9; /* suffix RK index */
    36	            unsigned int obj:9; /* object RK index */
    37	            unsigned int tt:5; /* object type */
    38	        } ss;
    39	        breal r;     /* for ETREAL */
    40	        bint i;      /* for ETINT */
    41	        bstring *s;  /* for ETSTRING */
    42	        bproto *p;  /* for ETPROTO */
    43	        int idx;     /* variable index */
    44	    } v;
    45	    int t; /* patch list of 'exit when true' */
    46	    int f; /* patch list of 'exit when false' */
    47	    bbyte not; /* not mark */
    48	    bbyte type;
    49	} bexpdesc;
    50	
    51	typedef struct bblockinfo {
    52	    struct bblockinfo *prev;
    53	    bbyte nactlocals; /* number of active local variables */
    54	    bbyte type;       /* block type mask */
    55	    bbyte hasupval;   /* has upvalue mark */
    56	    int breaklist;    /* break list */
    57	    int beginpc;      /* begin pc */
    58	    int continuelist; /* continue list */
    59	} bblockinfo;
    60	
    61	typedef struct bfuncinfo {
    62	    struct bfuncinfo *prev; /* outer function */
    63	    bproto *proto; /* the function prototype */
    64	    bblockinfo *binfo; /* block information */
    65	    struct blexer *lexer; /* the lexer pointer */
    66	    blist *local; /* local variable */
    67	    bmap *upval; /* upvalue variable */
    68	    bvector code; /* code vector */
    69	    bvector kvec; /* constants table */
    70	    bvector pvec; /* proto table */
    71	#if BE_DEBUG_RUNTIME_INFO /* debug information */
    72	    bvector linevec;
    73	#endif
    74	#if BE_DEBUG_VAR_INFO
    75	    bvector varvec;
    76	#endif
    77	    int pc; /* program count */
    78	    bbyte freereg; /* first free register */
    79	    bbyte flags; /* some flages */
    80	} bfuncinfo;
    81	
    82	/* code block type definitions */
    83	#define BLOCK_LOOP      1
    84	#define BLOCK_EXCEPT    2
    85	
    86	bclosure *be_parser_source(bvm *vm,
    87	    const char *fname, breader reader, void *data, bbool islocal);
    88	
    89	#endif

     8	#ifndef BE_VM_H
     9	#define BE_VM_H
    10	
    11	#include "be_object.h"
    12	
    13	#define comp_is_named_gbl(vm)       ((vm)->compopt & (1<<COMP_NAMED_GBL))
    14	#define comp_set_named_gbl(vm)      ((vm)->compopt |= (1<<COMP_NAMED_GBL))
    15	#define comp_clear_named_gbl(vm)    ((vm)->compopt &= ~(1<<COMP_NAMED_GBL))
    16	
    17	#define comp_is_strict(vm)       ((vm)->compopt & (1<<COMP_STRICT))
    18	#define comp_set_strict(vm)      ((vm)->compopt |= (1<<COMP_STRICT))
    19	#define comp_clear_strict(vm)    ((vm)->compopt &= ~(1<<COMP_STRICT))
    20	
    21	/* Compilation options */
    22	typedef enum {
    23	    COMP_NAMED_GBL = 0x00, /* compile with named globals */
    24	    COMP_STRICT = 0x01, /* compile with named globals */
    25	} compoptmask;
    26	
    27	typedef struct {
    28	    struct {
    29	        bmap *vtab; /* global variable index table */
    30	        bvector vlist; /* global variable list */
    31	    } global;
    32	    struct {
    33	        bmap *vtab; /* built-in variable index table */
    34	        bvector vlist; /* built-in variable list */
    35	    } builtin;
    36	} bglobaldesc;
    37	
    38	typedef struct {
    39	    bvalue *func; /* function register pointer */
    40	    bvalue *top; /* top register pointer */
    41	    bvalue *reg; /* base register pointer */
    42	    binstruction *ip; /* instruction pointer (only berry-function) */
    43	#if BE_USE_DEBUG_HOOK
    44	    blineinfo *lineinfo;
    45	#endif
    46	    int status;
    47	} bcallframe;
    48	
    49	struct bgc {
    50	    bgcobject *list; /* the GC-object list */
    51	    bgcobject *gray; /* the gray object list */
    52	    bgcobject *fixed; /* the fixed objecct list  */
    53	    size_t usage; /* the count of bytes currently allocated */
    54	    size_t threshold; /* he threshold of allocation for the next GC */
    55	    bbyte steprate; /* the rate of increase in the distribution between two GCs (percentage) */
    56	    bbyte status;
    57	};
    58	
    59	struct bstringtable {
    60	    bstring **table;
    61	    int count; /* string count */
    62	    int size;
    63	};
    64	
    65	struct bmoduledesc {
    66	    bmap *loaded; /* loaded module map */
    67	    blist *path; /* module load path list */
    68	};
    69	
    70	typedef struct {
    71	    bvalue func;
    72	    binstruction *ip;
    73	} bcallsnapshot;
    74	
    75	struct bupval {
    76	    bvalue* value;
    77	    union {
    78	        bvalue value;
    79	        struct bupval* next;
    80	    } u;
    81	    int refcnt;
    82	};
    83	
    84	struct bvm {
    85	    bglobaldesc gbldesc; /* global description */
    86	    bvalue *stack; /* stack space */
    87	    bvalue *stacktop; /* stack top register */
    88	    bupval *upvalist; /* open upvalue list */
    89	    bstack callstack; /* function call stack */
    90	    bstack exceptstack; /* exception stack */
    91	    bcallframe *cf; /* function call frame */
    92	    bvalue *reg; /* function base register */
    93	    bvalue *top; /* function top register */
    94	    binstruction *ip; /* function instruction pointer */
    95	    struct blongjmp *errjmp; /* error jump point */
    96	    bstack refstack; /* object reference stack */
    97	    struct bmoduledesc module; /* module description */
    98	    struct bstringtable strtab; /* short string table */
    99	    bstack tracestack; /* call state trace-stack */
   100	    bmap *ntvclass; /* native class table */
   101	    blist *registry; /* registry list */
   102	    struct bgc gc;
   103	    bbyte compopt; /* compilation options */
   104	#if BE_USE_OBSERVABILITY_HOOK
   105	    bobshook obshook;
   106	#endif
   107	#if BE_USE_DEBUG_HOOK
   108	    bvalue hook;
   109	    bbyte hookmask;
   110	#endif
   111	};
   112	
   113	#define NONE_FLAG           0
   114	#define BASE_FRAME          (1 << 0)
   115	#define PRIM_FUNC           (1 << 1)
   116	
   117	void be_dofunc(bvm *vm, bvalue *v, int argc);
   118	bbool be_value2bool(bvm *vm, bvalue *v);
   119	bbool be_vm_iseq(bvm *vm, bvalue *a, bvalue *b);
   120	bbool be_vm_isneq(bvm *vm, bvalue *a, bvalue *b);
   121	bbool be_vm_islt(bvm *vm, bvalue *a, bvalue *b);
   122	bbool be_vm_isle(bvm *vm, bvalue *a, bvalue *b);
   123	bbool be_vm_isgt(bvm *vm, bvalue *a, bvalue *b);

     8	#ifndef BE_OBJECT_H
     9	#define BE_OBJECT_H
    10	
    11	#include "berry.h"
    12	
    13	/* basic types, do not change value */
    14	#define BE_NONE         (-1)    /* unknown type */
    15	#define BE_COMPTR       (-2)    /* common pointer */
    16	#define BE_INDEX        (-3)    /* index for instance variable, previously BE_INT */
    17	#define BE_NIL          0
    18	#define BE_INT          1
    19	#define BE_REAL         2
    20	#define BE_BOOL         3
    21	#define BE_FUNCTION     4
    22	#define BE_STRING       5       /* from this type can be gced, see BE_GCOBJECT */
    23	#define BE_CLASS        6
    24	#define BE_INSTANCE     7
    25	#define BE_PROTO        8
    26	#define BE_LIST         9
    27	#define BE_MAP          10
    28	#define BE_MODULE       11
    29	#define BE_COMOBJ       12      /* common object */
    30	#define BE_NTVFUNC      ((0 << 5) | BE_FUNCTION)
    31	#define BE_CLOSURE      ((1 << 5) | BE_FUNCTION)
    32	#define BE_NTVCLOS      ((2 << 5) | BE_FUNCTION)
    33	
    34	#define array_count(a)   (sizeof(a) / sizeof((a)[0]))
    35	
    36	#define bcommon_header          \
    37	    struct bgcobject *next;     \
    38	    bbyte type;                 \
    39	    bbyte marked
    40	
    41	#define bstring_header          \
    42	    bcommon_header;             \
    43	    bbyte extra;                \
    44	    bbyte slen
    45	
    46	typedef struct bgcobject {
    47	    bcommon_header;
    48	} bgcobject;
    49	
    50	typedef struct bclosure bclosure;
    51	typedef struct bntvclos bntvclos;
    52	typedef struct bclass bclass;
    53	typedef struct binstance binstance;
    54	typedef struct blist blist;
    55	typedef struct bmap bmap;
    56	typedef struct bupval bupval;
    57	
    58	typedef uint32_t binstruction;
    59	
    60	typedef struct bstring {
    61	    bstring_header;
    62	} bstring;
    63	
    64	/* the definition of the vector and stack data structures.
    65	 * in fact, the stack is implemented by vector. */
    66	typedef struct bvector {
    67	    int capacity;   /* the number of elements that the vector can store */
    68	    int size;       /* the size of each element (bytes) */
    69	    int count;      /* number of elements of the vector */
    70	    void *data;     /* the data block pointer, if vector is empty,
    71	                       it will point to the first element */
    72	    void *end;      /* pointer to the last element, if the vector is empty,
    73	                       the end pointer will be smaller than the data pointer */
    74	} bvector, bstack;
    75	
    76	/* berry value data union, a berry value is always described
    77	 * by the data structure contained in the bvaldata union. */
    78	union bvaldata {
    79	    bbool b;        /* boolean */
    80	    breal r;        /* real number */
    81	    bint i;         /* integer number */
    82	    void *p;        /* object pointer */
    83	    const void *c;  /* const object pointer */
    84	    bstring *s;     /* string pointer */
    85	    bgcobject *gc;  /* GC object */
    86	    bntvfunc nf;    /* native C function */
    87	#ifdef __cplusplus
    88	    BE_CONSTEXPR bvaldata(bbool v) : b(v) {}
    89	    BE_CONSTEXPR bvaldata(breal v) : r(v) {}
    90	    BE_CONSTEXPR bvaldata(bint v) : i(v) {}
    91	    BE_CONSTEXPR bvaldata(void *v) : p(v) {}
    92	    BE_CONSTEXPR bvaldata(const void *v) : c(v) {}
    93	    BE_CONSTEXPR bvaldata(bntvfunc v) : nf(v) {}
    94	#endif
    95	};
    96	
    97	/* berry value. for simple types, the value of the data is stored,
    98	 * while the complex type stores a reference to the data. */
    99	typedef struct bvalue {
   100	    union bvaldata v; /* the value data */
   101	    int type;         /* the value type */
   102	} bvalue;
   103	
   104	typedef struct {
   105	#if BE_DEBUG_VAR_INFO
   106	    bstring *name;
   107	#endif
   108	    bbyte instack;
   109	    bbyte idx;
   110	} bupvaldesc;
   111	
   112	typedef struct {
   113	#if BE_DEBUG_RUNTIME_INFO > 1
   114	    uint16_t linenumber;
   115	    uint16_t endpc;
   116	#else
   117	    int linenumber;
   118	    int endpc;
   119	#endif
   120	} blineinfo;
   121	
   122	typedef struct {
   123	    bstring *name;
   124	#if BE_DEBUG_RUNTIME_INFO > 1
   125	    uint16_t beginpc;
   126	    uint16_t endpc;
   127	#else
   128	    int beginpc;
   129	    int endpc;
   130	#endif
   131	} bvarinfo;
   132	
   133	typedef struct bproto {
   134	    bcommon_header;
   135	    bbyte nstack; /* number of stack size by this function */
   136	    bbyte nupvals; /* upvalue count */
   137	    bbyte argc; /* argument count */
   138	    bbyte varg; /* variable argument position + 1 */
   139	    bgcobject *gray; /* for gc gray list */
   140	    bupvaldesc *upvals;
   141	    bvalue *ktab; /* constants table */
   142	    struct bproto **ptab; /* proto table */
   143	    binstruction *code; /* instructions sequence */
   144	    bstring *name; /* function name */
   145	    int codesize; /* code size */
   146	    int nconst; /* constants count */
   147	    int nproto; /* proto count */
   148	    bstring *source; /* source file name */
   149	#if BE_DEBUG_RUNTIME_INFO /* debug information */
   150	    blineinfo *lineinfo;
   151	    int nlineinfo;
   152	#endif
   153	#if BE_DEBUG_VAR_INFO
   154	    bvarinfo *varinfo;
   155	    int nvarinfo;
   156	#endif
   157	} bproto;
   158	
   159	/* berry closure */
   160	struct bclosure {
   161	    bcommon_header;
   162	    bbyte nupvals;
   163	    bgcobject *gray; /* for gc gray list */
   164	    bproto *proto;
   165	    bupval *upvals[1];
   166	};
   167	
   168	/* C native function or closure */
   169	struct bntvclos {
   170	    bcommon_header;
   171	    bbyte nupvals;
   172	    bgcobject *gray; /* for gc gray list */
   173	    bntvfunc f;
   174	};
   175	
   176	/* common object */
   177	typedef struct {
   178	    bcommon_header;
   179	    void *data;
   180	    bntvfunc destory;
   181	} bcommomobj;
   182	
   183	typedef const char* (*breader)(void*, size_t*);
   184	
   185	#define cast(_T, _v)            ((_T)(_v))
   186	#define cast_int(_v)            cast(int, _v)
   187	#define cast_bool(_v)           cast(bbool, _v)
   188	#define basetype(_t)            ((_t) & 0x1F)
   189	
   190	#define var_type(_v)            ((_v)->type)
   191	#define var_basetype(_v)        basetype((_v)->type)
   192	#define var_istype(_v, _t)      (var_type(_v) == _t)
   193	#define var_settype(_v, _t)     ((_v)->type = _t)

     8	#ifndef BE_PARSER_H
     9	#define BE_PARSER_H
    10	
    11	#include "be_object.h"
    12	#include "be_string.h"
    13	
    14	typedef enum {
    15	    ETVOID,    /* unknown (new variable or error) */
    16	    ETNIL,
    17	    ETBOOL,
    18	    ETREAL,
    19	    ETINT,
    20	    ETSTRING,
    21	    ETPROTO,
    22	    ETCONST,
    23	    ETLOCAL,    /* local variable, allocated until end of scope */
    24	    ETGLOBAL,   /* global by index number */
    25	    ETUPVAL,
    26	    ETMEMBER,   /* member accessor (by name) */
    27	    ETINDEX,    /* index accessor (ex array index) */
    28	    ETREG,      /* temporary register, can be freed if top of stack */
    29	    ETNGLOBAL   /* named global */
    30	} exptype_t;
    31	
    32	typedef struct {
    33	    union {
    34	        struct { /* for suffix */
    35	            unsigned int idx:9; /* suffix RK index */
    36	            unsigned int obj:9; /* object RK index */
    37	            unsigned int tt:5; /* object type */
    38	        } ss;
    39	        breal r;     /* for ETREAL */
    40	        bint i;      /* for ETINT */
    41	        bstring *s;  /* for ETSTRING */
    42	        bproto *p;  /* for ETPROTO */
    43	        int idx;     /* variable index */
    44	    } v;
    45	    int t; /* patch list of 'exit when true' */
    46	    int f; /* patch list of 'exit when false' */
    47	    bbyte not; /* not mark */
    48	    bbyte type;
    49	} bexpdesc;
    50	
    51	typedef struct bblockinfo {
    52	    struct bblockinfo *prev;
    53	    bbyte nactlocals; /* number of active local variables */
    54	    bbyte type;       /* block type mask */
    55	    bbyte hasupval;   /* has upvalue mark */
    56	    int breaklist;    /* break list */
    57	    int beginpc;      /* begin pc */
    58	    int continuelist; /* continue list */
    59	} bblockinfo;
    60	
    61	typedef struct bfuncinfo {
    62	    struct bfuncinfo *prev; /* outer function */
    63	    bproto *proto; /* the function prototype */
    64	    bblockinfo *binfo; /* block information */
    65	    struct blexer *lexer; /* the lexer pointer */
    66	    blist *local; /* local variable */
    67	    bmap *upval; /* upvalue variable */
    68	    bvector code; /* code vector */
    69	    bvector kvec; /* constants table */
    70	    bvector pvec; /* proto table */
    71	#if BE_DEBUG_RUNTIME_INFO /* debug information */
    72	    bvector linevec;
    73	#endif
    74	#if BE_DEBUG_VAR_INFO
    75	    bvector varvec;
    76	#endif
    77	    int pc; /* program count */
    78	    bbyte freereg; /* first free register */
    79	    bbyte flags; /* some flages */
    80	} bfuncinfo;
    81	
    82	/* code block type definitions */
    83	#define BLOCK_LOOP      1
    84	#define BLOCK_EXCEPT    2
    85	
    86	bclosure *be_parser_source(bvm *vm,
    87	    const char *fname, breader reader, void *data, bbool islocal);
    88	
    89	#endif
</output>

     8	#ifndef BE_VM_H
     9	#define BE_VM_H
    10	
    11	#include "be_object.h"
    12	
    13	#define comp_is_named_gbl(vm)       ((vm)->compopt & (1<<COMP_NAMED_GBL))
    14	#define comp_set_named_gbl(vm)      ((vm)->compopt |= (1<<COMP_NAMED_GBL))
    15	#define comp_clear_named_gbl(vm)    ((vm)->compopt &= ~(1<<COMP_NAMED_GBL))
    16	
    17	#define comp_is_strict(vm)       ((vm)->compopt & (1<<COMP_STRICT))
    18	#define comp_set_strict(vm)      ((vm)->compopt |= (1<<COMP_STRICT))
    19	#define comp_clear_strict(vm)    ((vm)->compopt &= ~(1<<COMP_STRICT))
    20	
    21	/* Compilation options */
    22	typedef enum {
    23	    COMP_NAMED_GBL = 0x00, /* compile with named globals */
    24	    COMP_STRICT = 0x01, /* compile with named globals */
    25	} compoptmask;
    26	
    27	typedef struct {
    28	    struct {
    29	        bmap *vtab; /* global variable index table */
    30	        bvector vlist; /* global variable list */
    31	    } global;
    32	    struct {
    33	        bmap *vtab; /* built-in variable index table */
    34	        bvector vlist; /* built-in variable list */
    35	    } builtin;
    36	} bglobaldesc;
    37	
    38	typedef struct {
    39	    bvalue *func; /* function register pointer */
    40	    bvalue *top; /* top register pointer */
    41	    bvalue *reg; /* base register pointer */
    42	    binstruction *ip; /* instruction pointer (only berry-function) */
    43	#if BE_USE_DEBUG_HOOK
    44	    blineinfo *lineinfo;
    45	#endif
    46	    int status;
    47	} bcallframe;
    48	
    49	struct bgc {
    50	    bgcobject *list; /* the GC-object list */
    51	    bgcobject *gray; /* the gray object list */
    52	    bgcobject *fixed; /* the fixed objecct list  */
    53	    size_t usage; /* the count of bytes currently allocated */
    54	    size_t threshold; /* he threshold of allocation for the next GC */
    55	    bbyte steprate; /* the rate of increase in the distribution between two GCs (percentage) */
    56	    bbyte status;
    57	};
    58	
    59	struct bstringtable {
    60	    bstring **table;
    61	    int count; /* string count */
    62	    int size;
    63	};
    64	
    65	struct bmoduledesc {
    66	    bmap *loaded; /* loaded module map */
    67	    blist *path; /* module load path list */
    68	};
    69	
    70	typedef struct {
    71	    bvalue func;
    72	    binstruction *ip;
    73	} bcallsnapshot;
    74	
    75	struct bupval {
    76	    bvalue* value;
    77	    union {
    78	        bvalue value;
    79	        struct bupval* next;
    80	    } u;
    81	    int refcnt;
    82	};
    83	
    84	struct bvm {
    85	    bglobaldesc gbldesc; /* global description */
    86	    bvalue *stack; /* stack space */
    87	    bvalue *stacktop; /* stack top register */
    88	    bupval *upvalist; /* open upvalue list */
    89	    bstack callstack; /* function call stack */
    90	    bstack exceptstack; /* exception stack */
    91	    bcallframe *cf; /* function call frame */
    92	    bvalue *reg; /* function base register */
    93	    bvalue *top; /* function top register */
    94	    binstruction *ip; /* function instruction pointer */
    95	    struct blongjmp *errjmp; /* error jump point */
    96	    bstack refstack; /* object reference stack */
    97	    struct bmoduledesc module; /* module description */
    98	    struct bstringtable strtab; /* short string table */
    99	    bstack tracestack; /* call state trace-stack */
   100	    bmap *ntvclass; /* native class table */
   101	    blist *registry; /* registry list */
   102	    struct bgc gc;
   103	    bbyte compopt; /* compilation options */
   104	#if BE_USE_OBSERVABILITY_HOOK
   105	    bobshook obshook;
   106	#endif
   107	#if BE_USE_DEBUG_HOOK
   108	    bvalue hook;
   109	    bbyte hookmask;
   110	#endif
   111	};
   112	
   113	#define NONE_FLAG           0
   114	#define BASE_FRAME          (1 << 0)
   115	#define PRIM_FUNC           (1 << 1)
   116	
   117	void be_dofunc(bvm *vm, bvalue *v, int argc);
   118	bbool be_value2bool(bvm *vm, bvalue *v);
   119	bbool be_vm_iseq(bvm *vm, bvalue *a, bvalue *b);
   120	bbool be_vm_isneq(bvm *vm, bvalue *a, bvalue *b);
   121	bbool be_vm_islt(bvm *vm, bvalue *a, bvalue *b);
   122	bbool be_vm_isle(bvm *vm, bvalue *a, bvalue *b);
   123	bbool be_vm_isgt(bvm *vm, bvalue *a, bvalue *b);

     1	/********************************************************************
     2	** Copyright (c) 2018-2020 Guan Wenliang
     3	** This file is part of the Berry default interpreter.
     4	** skiars@qq.com, https://github.com/Skiars/berry

     7	********************************************************************/
     8	#ifndef BE_OBJECT_H
     9	#define BE_OBJECT_H
    10	
    11	#include "berry.h"
    12	
    13	/* basic types, do not change value */
    14	#define BE_NONE         (-1)    /* unknown type */
    15	#define BE_COMPTR       (-2)    /* common pointer */
    16	#define BE_INDEX        (-3)    /* index for instance variable, previously BE_INT */
    17	#define BE_NIL          0
    18	#define BE_INT          1
    19	#define BE_REAL         2
    20	#define BE_BOOL         3
    21	#define BE_FUNCTION     4
    22	#define BE_STRING       5       /* from this type can be gced, see BE_GCOBJECT */
    23	#define BE_CLASS        6
    24	#define BE_INSTANCE     7
    25	#define BE_PROTO        8
    26	#define BE_LIST         9
    27	#define BE_MAP          10
    28	#define BE_MODULE       11
    29	#define BE_COMOBJ       12      /* common object */
    30	#define BE_NTVFUNC      ((0 << 5) | BE_FUNCTION)
    31	#define BE_CLOSURE      ((1 << 5) | BE_FUNCTION)
    32	#define BE_NTVCLOS      ((2 << 5) | BE_FUNCTION)
    33	
    34	#define array_count(a)   (sizeof(a) / sizeof((a)[0]))
    35	
    36	#define bcommon_header          \
    37	    struct bgcobject *next;     \
    38	    bbyte type;                 \
    39	    bbyte marked
    40	
    41	#define bstring_header          \
    42	    bcommon_header;             \
    43	    bbyte extra;                \
    44	    bbyte slen
    45	
    46	typedef struct bgcobject {
    47	    bcommon_header;
    48	} bgcobject;
    49	
    50	typedef struct bclosure bclosure;
    51	typedef struct bntvclos bntvclos;
    52	typedef struct bclass bclass;
    53	typedef struct binstance binstance;
    54	typedef struct blist blist;
    55	typedef struct bmap bmap;
    56	typedef struct bupval bupval;
    57	
    58	typedef uint32_t binstruction;
    59	
    60	typedef struct bstring {
    61	    bstring_header;
    62	} bstring;
    63	
    64	/* the definition of the vector and stack data structures.
    65	 * in fact, the stack is implemented by vector. */
    66	typedef struct bvector {
    67	    int capacity;   /* the number of elements that the vector can store */
    68	    int size;       /* the size of each element (bytes) */
    69	    int count;      /* number of elements of the vector */
    70	    void *data;     /* the data block pointer, if vector is empty,
    71	                       it will point to the first element */
    72	    void *end;      /* pointer to the last element, if the vector is empty,
    73	                       the end pointer will be smaller than the data pointer */
    74	} bvector, bstack;
    75	
    76	/* berry value data union, a berry value is always described
    77	 * by the data structure contained in the bvaldata union. */
    78	union bvaldata {
    79	    bbool b;        /* boolean */
    80	    breal r;        /* real number */
    81	    bint i;         /* integer number */
    82	    void *p;        /* object pointer */
    83	    const void *c;  /* const object pointer */
    84	    bstring *s;     /* string pointer */
    85	    bgcobject *gc;  /* GC object */
    86	    bntvfunc nf;    /* native C function */
    87	#ifdef __cplusplus
    88	    BE_CONSTEXPR bvaldata(bbool v) : b(v) {}
    89	    BE_CONSTEXPR bvaldata(breal v) : r(v) {}
    90	    BE_CONSTEXPR bvaldata(bint v) : i(v) {}
    91	    BE_CONSTEXPR bvaldata(void *v) : p(v) {}
    92	    BE_CONSTEXPR bvaldata(const void *v) : c(v) {}
    93	    BE_CONSTEXPR bvaldata(bntvfunc v) : nf(v) {}
    94	#endif
    95	};
    96	
    97	/* berry value. for simple types, the value of the data is stored,
    98	 * while the complex type stores a reference to the data. */
    99	typedef struct bvalue {
   100	    union bvaldata v; /* the value data */
   101	    int type;         /* the value type */
   102	} bvalue;
   103	
   104	typedef struct {
   105	#if BE_DEBUG_VAR_INFO
   106	    bstring *name;
   107	#endif
   108	    bbyte instack;
   109	    bbyte idx;
   110	} bupvaldesc;
   111	
   112	typedef struct {
   113	#if BE_DEBUG_RUNTIME_INFO > 1
   114	    uint16_t linenumber;
   115	    uint16_t endpc;
   116	#else
   117	    int linenumber;
   118	    int endpc;
   119	#endif
   120	} blineinfo;
   121	
   122	typedef struct {
   123	    bstring *name;
   124	#if BE_DEBUG_RUNTIME_INFO > 1
   125	    uint16_t beginpc;
   126	    uint16_t endpc;
   127	#else
   128	    int beginpc;
   129	    int endpc;
   130	#endif
   131	} bvarinfo;
   132	
   133	typedef struct bproto {
   134	    bcommon_header;
   135	    bbyte nstack; /* number of stack size by this function */
   136	    bbyte nupvals; /* upvalue count */
   137	    bbyte argc; /* argument count */
   138	    bbyte varg; /* variable argument position + 1 */
   139	    bgcobject *gray; /* for gc gray list */
   140	    bupvaldesc *upvals;
   141	    bvalue *ktab; /* constants table */
   142	    struct bproto **ptab; /* proto table */
   143	    binstruction *code; /* instructions sequence */
   144	    bstring *name; /* function name */

   147	    int nproto; /* proto count */
   148	    bstring *source; /* source file name */
   149	#if BE_DEBUG_RUNTIME_INFO /* debug information */
   150	    blineinfo *lineinfo;
   151	    int nlineinfo;
   152	#endif
   153	#if BE_DEBUG_VAR_INFO
   154	    bvarinfo *varinfo;
   155	    int nvarinfo;
   156	#endif
   157	} bproto;
   158	
   159	/* berry closure */
   160	struct bclosure {
   161	    bcommon_header;
   162	    bbyte nupvals;
   163	    bgcobject *gray; /* for gc gray list */
   164	    bproto *proto;
   165	    bupval *upvals[1];
   166	};
   167	
   168	/* C native function or closure */
   169	struct bntvclos {
   170	    bcommon_header;
   171	    bbyte nupvals;
   172	    bgcobject *gray; /* for gc gray list */
   173	    bntvfunc f;
   174	};
   175	
   176	/* common object */
   177	typedef struct {
   178	    bcommon_header;
   179	    void *data;
   180	    bntvfunc destory;
   181	} bcommomobj;
   182	
   183	typedef const char* (*breader)(void*, size_t*);
   184	
   185	#define cast(_T, _v)            ((_T)(_v))

   190	#define var_type(_v)            ((_v)->type)

   192	#define var_istype(_v, _t)      (var_type(_v) == _t)
   193	#define var_settype(_v, _t)     ((_v)->type = _t)
   194	#define var_setobj(_v, _t, _o)  { (_v)->v.p = _o; var_settype(_v, _t); }
   195	
   196	#define var_isnil(_v)           var_istype(_v, BE_NIL)

   211	#define var_isindex(_v)         var_istype(_v, BE_INDEX)
   212	#define var_iscomptr(_v)        var_istype(_v, BE_COMPTR)
   213	#define var_isnumber(_v)        (var_isint(_v) || var_isreal(_v))
   214	
   215	#define var_setnil(_v)          var_settype(_v, BE_NIL)
   216	#define var_setval(_v, _s)      (*(_v) = *(_s))
   217	#define var_setbool(_v, _b)     { var_settype(_v, BE_BOOL); (_v)->v.b = (bbool)(_b); }
   218	#define var_setint(_v, _i)      { var_settype(_v, BE_INT); (_v)->v.i = (_i); }
   219	#define var_setreal(_v, _r)     { var_settype(_v, BE_REAL); (_v)->v.r = (_r); }
   220	#define var_setstr(_v, _s)      var_setobj(_v, BE_STRING, _s)
   221	#define var_setinstance(_v, _o) var_setobj(_v, BE_INSTANCE, _o)
   222	#define var_setclass(_v, _o)    var_setobj(_v, BE_CLASS, _o)

   224	#define var_setntvclos(_v, _o)  var_setobj(_v, BE_NTVCLOS, _o)
   225	#define var_setntvfunc(_v, _o)  { (_v)->v.nf = (_o); var_settype(_v, BE_NTVFUNC); }
   226	#define var_setlist(_v, _o)     var_setobj(_v, BE_LIST, _o)
   227	#define var_setmap(_v, _o)      var_setobj(_v, BE_MAP, _o)
   228	#define var_setmodule(_v, _o)   var_setobj(_v, BE_MODULE, _o)
   229	#define var_setindex(_v, _i)      { var_settype(_v, BE_INDEX); (_v)->v.i = (_i); }
   230	#define var_setproto(_v, _o)    var_setobj(_v, BE_PROTO, _o)
   231	
   232	#define var_tobool(_v)          ((_v)->v.b)
   233	#define var_toint(_v)           ((_v)->v.i)
   234	#define var_toreal(_v)          ((_v)->v.r)
   235	#define var_tostr(_v)           ((_v)->v.s)
   236	#define var_togc(_v)            ((_v)->v.gc)
   237	#define var_toobj(_v)           ((_v)->v.p)

    14	typedef enum {

    16	    ETNIL,
    17	    ETBOOL,
    18	    ETREAL,
    19	    ETINT,
    20	    ETSTRING,
    21	    ETPROTO,
    22	    ETCONST,
    23	    ETLOCAL,    /* local variable, allocated until end of scope */
    24	    ETGLOBAL,   /* global by index number */
    25	    ETUPVAL,
    26	    ETMEMBER,   /* member accessor (by name) */
    27	    ETINDEX,    /* index accessor (ex array index) */
    28	    ETREG,      /* temporary register, can be freed if top of stack */
    29	    ETNGLOBAL   /* named global */
    30	} exptype_t;
    31	
    32	typedef struct {
    33	    union {
    34	        struct { /* for suffix */
    35	            unsigned int idx:9; /* suffix RK index */
    36	            unsigned int obj:9; /* object RK index */
    37	            unsigned int tt:5; /* object type */
    38	        } ss;
    39	        breal r;     /* for ETREAL */
    40	        bint i;      /* for ETINT */
    41	        bstring *s;  /* for ETSTRING */
    42	        bproto *p;  /* for ETPROTO */
    43	        int idx;     /* variable index */
    44	    } v;
    45	    int t; /* patch list of 'exit when true' */
    46	    int f; /* patch list of 'exit when false' */
    47	    bbyte not; /* not mark */
    48	    bbyte type;
    49	} bexpdesc;
    50	
    51	typedef struct bblockinfo {
    52	    struct bblockinfo *prev;
    53	    bbyte nactlocals; /* number of active local variables */

    55	    bbyte hasupval;   /* has upvalue mark */
    56	    int breaklist;    /* break list */
    57	    int beginpc;      /* begin pc */
    58	    int continuelist; /* continue list */
    59	} bblockinfo;
    60	
    61	typedef struct bfuncinfo {
    62	    struct bfuncinfo *prev; /* outer function */
    63	    bproto *proto; /* the function prototype */
    64	    bblockinfo *binfo; /* block information */
    65	    struct blexer *lexer; /* the lexer pointer */
    66	    blist *local; /* local variable */
    67	    bmap *upval; /* upvalue variable */

    80	} bfuncinfo;
    81

    83	#define BLOCK_LOOP      1
    84	#define BLOCK_EXCEPT    2
    85	
    86	bclosure *be_parser_source(bvm *vm,

    89	#endif

     8	#ifndef BE_VM_H
     9	#define BE_VM_H
    10	
    11	#include "be_object.h"
    12	
    13	#define comp_is_named_gbl(vm)       ((vm)->compopt & (1<<COMP_NAMED_GBL))
    14	#define comp_set_named_gbl(vm)      ((vm)->compopt |= (1<<COMP_NAMED_GBL))
    15	#define comp_clear_named_gbl(vm)    ((vm)->compopt &= ~(1<<COMP_NAMED_GBL))
    16	
    17	#define comp_is_strict(vm)       ((vm)->compopt & (1<<COMP_STRICT))
    18	#define comp_set_strict(vm)      ((vm)->compopt |= (1<<COMP_STRICT))
    19	#define comp_clear_strict(vm)    ((vm)->compopt &= ~(1<<COMP_STRICT))
    20	
    21	/* Compilation options */
    22	typedef enum {
    23	    COMP_NAMED_GBL = 0x00, /* compile with named globals */
    24	    COMP_STRICT = 0x01, /* compile with named globals */
    25	} compoptmask;
    26	
    27	typedef struct {
    28	    struct {
    29	        bmap *vtab; /* global variable index table */
    30	        bvector vlist; /* global variable list */
    31	    } global;
    32	    struct {
    33	        bmap *vtab; /* built-in variable index table */
    34	        bvector vlist; /* built-in variable list */
    35	    } builtin;
    36	} bglobaldesc;
    37	
    38	typedef struct {
    39	    bvalue *func; /* function register pointer */
    40	    bvalue *top; /* top register pointer */
    41	    bvalue *reg; /* base register pointer */
    42	    binstruction *ip; /* instruction pointer (only berry-function) */
    43	#if BE_USE_DEBUG_HOOK
    44	    blineinfo *lineinfo;
    45	#endif
    46	    int status;
    47	} bcallframe;
    48	
    49	struct bgc {
    50	    bgcobject *list; /* the GC-object list */
    51	    bgcobject *gray; /* the gray object list */
    52	    bgcobject *fixed; /* the fixed objecct list  */
    53	    size_t usage; /* the count of bytes currently allocated */
    54	    size_t threshold; /* he threshold of allocation for the next GC */
    55	    bbyte steprate; /* the rate of increase in the distribution between two GCs (percentage) */
    56	    bbyte status;
    57	};
    58	
    59	struct bstringtable {
    60	    bstring **table;
    61	    int count; /* string count */
    62	    int size;
    63	};
    64	
    65	struct bmoduledesc {
    66	    bmap *loaded; /* loaded module map */
    67	    blist *path; /* module load path list */
    68	};
    69	
    70	typedef struct {
    71	    bvalue func;
    72	    binstruction *ip;
    73	} bcallsnapshot;
    74	
    75	struct bupval {
    76	    bvalue* value;
    77	    union {
    78	        bvalue value;
    79	        struct bupval* next;
    80	    } u;
    81	    int refcnt;
    82	};
    83	
    84	struct bvm {
    85	    bglobaldesc gbldesc; /* global description */
    86	    bvalue *stack; /* stack space */
    87	    bvalue *stacktop; /* stack top register */
    88	    bupval *upvalist; /* open upvalue list */
    89	    bstack callstack; /* function call stack */
    90	    bstack exceptstack; /* exception stack */
    91	    bcallframe *cf; /* function call frame */
    92	    bvalue *reg; /* function base register */
    93	    bvalue *top; /* function top register */
    94	    binstruction *ip; /* function instruction pointer */
    95	    struct blongjmp *errjmp; /* error jump point */
    96	    bstack refstack; /* object reference stack */
    97	    struct bmoduledesc module; /* module description */
    98	    struct bstringtable strtab; /* short string table */
    99	    bstack tracestack; /* call state trace-stack */
   100	    bmap *ntvclass; /* native class table */
   101	    blist *registry; /* registry list */
   102	    struct bgc gc;
   103	    bbyte compopt; /* compilation options */
   104	#if BE_USE_OBSERVABILITY_HOOK
   105	    bobshook obshook;
   106	#endif
   107	#if BE_USE_DEBUG_HOOK
   108	    bvalue hook;
   109	    bbyte hookmask;
   110	#endif
   111	};
   112	
   113	#define NONE_FLAG           0
   114	#define BASE_FRAME          (1 << 0)
   115	#define PRIM_FUNC           (1 << 1)
   116	
   117	void be_dofunc(bvm *vm, bvalue *v, int argc);
   118	bbool be_value2bool(bvm *vm, bvalue *v);

     8	#ifndef BE_OBJECT_H
     9	#define BE_OBJECT_H
    10	
    11	#include "berry.h"
    12	
    13	/* basic types, do not change value */
    14	#define BE_NONE         (-1)    /* unknown type */
    15	#define BE_COMPTR       (-2)    /* common pointer */
    16	#define BE_INDEX        (-3)    /* index for instance variable, previously BE_INT */
    17	#define BE_NIL          0
    18	#define BE_INT          1
    19	#define BE_REAL         2
    20	#define BE_BOOL         3
    21	#define BE_FUNCTION     4
    22	#define BE_STRING       5       /* from this type can be gced, see BE_GCOBJECT */
    23	#define BE_CLASS        6
    24	#define BE_INSTANCE     7
    25	#define BE_PROTO        8
    26	#define BE_LIST         9
    27	#define BE_MAP          10
    28	#define BE_MODULE       11
    29	#define BE_COMOBJ       12      /* common object */
    30	#define BE_NTVFUNC      ((0 << 5) | BE_FUNCTION)
    31	#define BE_CLOSURE      ((1 << 5) | BE_FUNCTION)
    32	#define BE_NTVCLOS      ((2 << 5) | BE_FUNCTION)
    33	
    34	#define array_count(a)   (sizeof(a) / sizeof((a)[0]))
    35	
    36	#define bcommon_header          \
    37	    struct bgcobject *next;     \
    38	    bbyte type;                 \
    39	    bbyte marked
    40	
    41	#define bstring_header          \
    42	    bcommon_header;             \
    43	    bbyte extra;                \
    44	    bbyte slen
    45	
    46	typedef struct bgcobject {
    47	    bcommon_header;
    48	} bgcobject;
    49	
    50	typedef struct bclosure bclosure;
    51	typedef struct bntvclos bntvclos;
    52	typedef struct bclass bclass;
    53	typedef struct binstance binstance;
    54	typedef struct blist blist;
    55	typedef struct bmap bmap;
    56	typedef struct bupval bupval;
    57	
    58	typedef uint32_t binstruction;
    59	
    60	typedef struct bstring {
    61	    bstring_header;
    62	} bstring;
    63	
    64	/* the definition of the vector and stack data structures.
    65	 * in fact, the stack is implemented by vector. */
    66	typedef struct bvector {
    67	    int capacity;   /* the number of elements that the vector can store */
    68	    int size;       /* the size of each element (bytes) */
    69	    int count;      /* number of elements of the vector */
    70	    void *data;     /* the data block pointer, if vector is empty,
    71	                       it will point to the first element */
    72	    void *end;      /* pointer to the last element, if the vector is empty,
    73	                       the end pointer will be smaller than the data pointer */
    74	} bvector, bstack;
    75	
    76	/* berry value data union, a berry value is always described
    77	 * by the data structure contained in the bvaldata union. */
    78	union bvaldata {
    79	    bbool b;        /* boolean */
    80	    breal r;        /* real number */
    81	    bint i;         /* integer number */
    82	    void *p;        /* object pointer */
    83	    const void *c;  /* const object pointer */
    84	    bstring *s;     /* string pointer */
    85	    bgcobject *gc;  /* GC object */
    86	    bntvfunc nf;    /* native C function */
    87	#ifdef __cplusplus
    88	    BE_CONSTEXPR bvaldata(bbool v) : b(v) {}
    89	    BE_CONSTEXPR bvaldata(breal v) : r(v) {}
    90	    BE_CONSTEXPR bvaldata(bint v) : i(v) {}
    91	    BE_CONSTEXPR bvaldata(void *v) : p(v) {}
    92	    BE_CONSTEXPR bvaldata(const void *v) : c(v) {}
    93	    BE_CONSTEXPR bvaldata(bntvfunc v) : nf(v) {}
    94	#endif
    95	};
    96	
    97	/* berry value. for simple types, the value of the data is stored,
    98	 * while the complex type stores a reference to the data. */
    99	typedef struct bvalue {
   100	    union bvaldata v; /* the value data */
   101	    int type;         /* the value type */
   102	} bvalue;
   103	
   104	typedef struct {
   105	#if BE_DEBUG_VAR_INFO
   106	    bstring *name;
   107	#endif
   108	    bbyte instack;
   109	    bbyte idx;
   110	} bupvaldesc;
   111	
   112	typedef struct {
   113	#if BE_DEBUG_RUNTIME_INFO > 1
   114	    uint16_t linenumber;
   115	    uint16_t endpc;
   116	#else
   117	    int linenumber;
   118	    int endpc;
   119	#endif
   120	} blineinfo;
   121	
   122	typedef struct {
   123	    bstring *name;
   124	#if BE_DEBUG_RUNTIME_INFO > 1
   125	    uint16_t beginpc;
   126	    uint16_t endpc;
   127	#else
   128	    int beginpc;
   129	    int endpc;
   130	#endif
   131	} bvarinfo;
   132	
   133	typedef struct bproto {
   134	    bcommon_header;
   135	    bbyte nstack; /* number of stack size by this function */
   136	    bbyte nupvals; /* upvalue count */
   137	    bbyte argc; /* argument count */
   138	    bbyte varg; /* variable argument position + 1 */
   139	    bgcobject *gray; /* for gc gray list */
   140	    bupvaldesc *upvals;
   141	    bvalue *ktab; /* constants table */
   142	    struct bproto **ptab; /* proto table */
   143	    binstruction *code; /* instructions sequence */
   144	    bstring *name; /* function name */
   145	    int codesize; /* code size */
   146	    int nconst; /* constants count */
   147	    int nproto; /* proto count */
   148	    bstring *source; /* source file name */
   149	#if BE_DEBUG_RUNTIME_INFO /* debug information */
   150	    blineinfo *lineinfo;
   151	    int nlineinfo;
   152	#endif
   153	#if BE_DEBUG_VAR_INFO
   154	    bvarinfo *varinfo;
   155	    int nvarinfo;
   156	#endif
   157	} bproto;
   158	
   159	/* berry closure */
   160	struct bclosure {
   161	    bcommon_header;
   162	    bbyte nupvals;
   163	    bgcobject *gray; /* for gc gray list */
   164	    bproto *proto;
   165	    bupval *upvals[1];
   166	};
   167	
   168	/* C native function or closure */
   169	struct bntvclos {
   170	    bcommon_header;
   171	    bbyte nupvals;
   172	    bgcobject *gray; /* for gc gray list */
   173	    bntvfunc f;
   174	};
   175	
   176	/* common object */
   177	typedef struct {
   178	    bcommon_header;
   179	    void *data;
   180	    bntvfunc destory;
   181	} bcommomobj;
   182	
   183	typedef const char* (*breader)(void*, size_t*);
   184	
   185	#define cast(_T, _v)            ((_T)(_v))
   186	#define cast_int(_v)            cast(int, _v)
   187	#define cast_bool(_v)           cast(bbool, _v)
   188	#define basetype(_t)            ((_t) & 0x1F)
   189	
   190	#define var_type(_v)            ((_v)->type)
   191	#define var_basetype(_v)        basetype((_v)->type)
   192	#define var_istype(_v, _t)      (var_type(_v) == _t)
   193	#define var_settype(_v, _t)     ((_v)->type = _t)
   194	#define var_setobj(_v, _t, _o)  { (_v)->v.p = _o; var_settype(_v, _t); }
   195	
   196	#define var_isnil(_v)           var_istype(_v, BE_NIL)
   197	#define var_isbool(_v)          var_istype(_v, BE_BOOL)
   198	#define var_isint(_v)           var_istype(_v, BE_INT)
   199	#define var_isreal(_v)          var_istype(_v, BE_REAL)
   200	#define var_isstr(_v)           var_istype(_v, BE_STRING)
   201	#define var_isclosure(_v)       var_istype(_v, BE_CLOSURE)
   202	#define var_isntvclos(_v)       var_istype(_v, BE_NTVCLOS)
   203	#define var_isntvfunc(_v)       var_istype(_v, BE_NTVFUNC)
   204	#define var_isfunction(_v)      (var_basetype(_v) == BE_FUNCTION)
   205	#define var_isproto(_v)         var_istype(_v, BE_PROTO)
   206	#define var_isclass(_v)         var_istype(_v, BE_CLASS)
   207	#define var_isinstance(_v)      var_istype(_v, BE_INSTANCE)
   208	#define var_islist(_v)          var_istype(_v, BE_LIST)
   209	#define var_ismap(_v)           var_istype(_v, BE_MAP)
   210	#define var_ismodule(_v)        var_istype(_v, BE_MODULE)
   211	#define var_isindex(_v)         var_istype(_v, BE_INDEX)
   212	#define var_iscomptr(_v)        var_istype(_v, BE_COMPTR)
   213	#define var_isnumber(_v)        (var_isint(_v) || var_isreal(_v))
   214	
   215	#define var_setnil(_v)          var_settype(_v, BE_NIL)
   216	#define var_setval(_v, _s)      (*(_v) = *(_s))
   217	#define var_setbool(_v, _b)     { var_settype(_v, BE_BOOL); (_v)->v.b = (bbool)(_b); }
   218	#define var_setint(_v, _i)      { var_settype(_v, BE_INT); (_v)->v.i = (_i); }
   219	#define var_setreal(_v, _r)     { var_settype(_v, BE_REAL); (_v)->v.r = (_r); }
   220	#define var_setstr(_v, _s)      var_setobj(_v, BE_STRING, _s)
   221	#define var_setinstance(_v, _o) var_setobj(_v, BE_INSTANCE, _o)
   222	#define var_setclass(_v, _o)    var_setobj(_v, BE_CLASS, _o)
   223	#define var_setclosure(_v, _o)  var_setobj(_v, BE_CLOSURE, _o)
   224	#define var_setntvclos(_v, _o)  var_setobj(_v, BE_NTVCLOS, _o)
   225	#define var_setntvfunc(_v, _o)  { (_v)->v.nf = (_o); var_settype(_v, BE_NTVFUNC); }
   226	#define var_setlist(_v, _o)     var_setobj(_v, BE_LIST, _o)
   227	#define var_setmap(_v, _o)      var_setobj(_v, BE_MAP, _o)
   228	#define var_setmodule(_v, _o)   var_setobj(_v, BE_MODULE, _o)
   229	#define var_setindex(_v, _i)      { var_settype(_v, BE_INDEX); (_v)->v.i = (_i); }
   230	#define var_setproto(_v, _o)    var_setobj(_v, BE_PROTO, _o)
   231	
   232	#define var_tobool(_v)          ((_v)->v.b)
   233	#define var_toint(_v)           ((_v)->v.i)
   234	#define var_toreal(_v)          ((_v)->v.r)
   235	#define var_tostr(_v)           ((_v)->v.s)
   236	#define var_togc(_v)            ((_v)->v.gc)
   237	#define var_toobj(_v)           ((_v)->v.p)
   238	#define var_tontvfunc(_v)       ((_v)->v.nf)
   239	#define var_toidx(_v)           cast_int(var_toint(_v))
   240	
   241	const char* be_vtype2str(bvalue *v);
   242	bvalue* be_indexof(bvm *vm, int idx);
   243	void be_commonobj_delete(bvm *vm, bgcobject *obj);
   244	
   245	#endif
</output>

     8	#ifndef BE_EXEC_H
     9	#define BE_EXEC_H
    10	
    11	#include "be_object.h"
    12	#include <setjmp.h>
    13	
    14	/* protected-call function */
    15	typedef void (*bpfunc)(bvm *vm, void *data);
    16	
    17	#if BE_DEBUG
    18	bvalue* be_incrtop(bvm *vm);
    19	#else
    20	/* increase top register */
    21	#define be_incrtop(vm)          ((vm)->top++)
    22	#endif
    23	
    24	#define be_stackpop(vm, n)      ((vm)->top -= (n))
    25	
    26	/* in MinGW-w64, setjmp / longjmp may be broken,
    27	 * so here is replaced by __builtin version. */
    28	#if defined(__GNUC__) && defined(__MINGW32__)
    29	  #define be_setjmp(env)        __builtin_setjmp(env)
    30	  #define be_longjmp(env, v)    __builtin_longjmp(env, v)
    31	#else
    32	  #define be_setjmp(env)        setjmp(env)
    33	  #define be_longjmp(env, v)    longjmp(env, v)
    34	#endif
    35	
    36	typedef jmp_buf bjmpbuf;
    37	
    38	struct blongjmp {
    39	    bjmpbuf b;
    40	    struct blongjmp *prev;
    41	    volatile int status; /* error code */
    42	};
    43	
    44	struct bexecptframe {
    45	    struct blongjmp errjmp; /* long jump information */
    46	    int depth; /* function call stack depth */
    47	    binstruction *ip; /* instruction pointer */
    48	};
    49	
    50	void be_throw(bvm *vm, int errorcode);
    51	int be_execprotected(bvm *vm, bpfunc f, void *data);
    52	int be_protectedparser(bvm *vm, const char *fname,
    53	    breader reader, void *data, bbool islocal);
    54	int be_protectedcall(bvm *vm, bvalue *v, int argc);
    55	void be_stackpush(bvm *vm);
    56	void be_stack_expansion(bvm *vm, int n);
    57	void be_except_block_setup(bvm *vm);
    58	void be_except_block_resume(bvm *vm);
    59	void be_except_block_close(bvm *vm, int count);
    60	void be_save_stacktrace(bvm *vm);
    61	
    62	#endif

     8	#ifndef BE_PARSER_H
     9	#define BE_PARSER_H
    10	
    11	#include "be_object.h"
    12	#include "be_string.h"
    13	
    14	typedef enum {
    15	    ETVOID,    /* unknown (new variable or error) */
    16	    ETNIL,
    17	    ETBOOL,
    18	    ETREAL,
    19	    ETINT,
    20	    ETSTRING,
    21	    ETPROTO,
    22	    ETCONST,
    23	    ETLOCAL,    /* local variable, allocated until end of scope */
    24	    ETGLOBAL,   /* global by index number */
    25	    ETUPVAL,
    26	    ETMEMBER,   /* member accessor (by name) */
    27	    ETINDEX,    /* index accessor (ex array index) */
    28	    ETREG,      /* temporary register, can be freed if top of stack */
    29	    ETNGLOBAL   /* named global */
    30	} exptype_t;
    31	
    32	typedef struct {
    33	    union {
    34	        struct { /* for suffix */
    35	            unsigned int idx:9; /* suffix RK index */
    36	            unsigned int obj:9; /* object RK index */
    37	            unsigned int tt:5; /* object type */
    38	        } ss;
    39	        breal r;     /* for ETREAL */
    40	        bint i;      /* for ETINT */
    41	        bstring *s;  /* for ETSTRING */
    42	        bproto *p;  /* for ETPROTO */
    43	        int idx;     /* variable index */
    44	    } v;
    45	    int t; /* patch list of 'exit when true' */
    46	    int f; /* patch list of 'exit when false' */
    47	    bbyte not; /* not mark */
    48	    bbyte type;
    49	} bexpdesc;
    50	
    51	typedef struct bblockinfo {
    52	    struct bblockinfo *prev;
    53	    bbyte nactlocals; /* number of active local variables */
    54	    bbyte type;       /* block type mask */
    55	    bbyte hasupval;   /* has upvalue mark */
    56	    int breaklist;    /* break list */
    57	    int beginpc;      /* begin pc */
    58	    int continuelist; /* continue list */
    59	} bblockinfo;
    60	
    61	typedef struct bfuncinfo {
    62	    struct bfuncinfo *prev; /* outer function */
    63	    bproto *proto; /* the function prototype */
    64	    bblockinfo *binfo; /* block information */
    65	    struct blexer *lexer; /* the lexer pointer */
    66	    blist *local; /* local variable */
    67	    bmap *upval; /* upvalue variable */
    68	    bvector code; /* code vector */
    69	    bvector kvec; /* constants table */
    70	    bvector pvec; /* proto table */
    71	#if BE_DEBUG_RUNTIME_INFO /* debug information */
    72	    bvector linevec;
    73	#endif
    74	#if BE_DEBUG_VAR_INFO
    75	    bvector varvec;
    76	#endif
    77	    int pc; /* program count */
    78	    bbyte freereg; /* first free register */
    79	    bbyte flags; /* some flages */
    80	} bfuncinfo;
    81	
    82	/* code block type definitions */
    83	#define BLOCK_LOOP      1
    84	#define BLOCK_EXCEPT    2
    85	
    86	bclosure *be_parser_source(bvm *vm,
    87	    const char *fname, breader reader, void *data, bbool islocal);
    88	
    89	#endif

     1	/********************************************************************
     2	** Copyright (c) 2018-2020 Guan Wenliang
     3	** This file is part of the Berry default interpreter.
     4	** skiars@qq.com, https://github.com/Skiars/berry
